#+TITLE: Python LP Caveats
#+SUBTITLE: org-babel-tangle conventions for Python literate programming
#+OPTIONS: toc:nil num:nil

Operational details for editing Python LP =.org= files and their
tangle outputs. Each entry: *trap* → *symptom* → *workaround*.

* NEVER edit the tangled =.py= directly

*Trap.* Python files generated by =org-babel-tangle= are *outputs*,
not sources. Direct edits get overwritten by the next tangle.

*Symptom.* You edit =python/<pkg>/foo.py= directly, run tests, they
pass. Next time someone runs =make tangle=, your edit vanishes
silently because the owning =.org= section still says the old thing.

*Workaround.* Always edit the owning =.org= section. The
=block-tangled-edit.sh= PreToolUse hook enforces this — Edit / Write
/ MultiEdit on a tangle-output =.py= returns exit 2 with a friendly
message pointing at the owning =.org= source. Consult
=docs/tangle-map-schema.org= for the reverse-lookup mechanism.

* Required file-local-vars line (poly-org users)

*Trap.* Without the formatter file-local-var, tangle emits raw
blocks; the diff vs. the source-of-truth =.py= drifts the moment
any block diverges from canonical ruff format.

*Symptom.* =git diff python/= shows formatter-only changes after
re-tangling. =make check-tangle-drift= flags drift even though the
behaviour hasn't changed.

*Workaround.* Every Python-tangling =.org= file opens with:

#+begin_example
,# -*- Mode: POLY-ORG; indent-tabs-mode: nil; literate-org-py-formatter: ruff;  -*- ---
#+end_example

(Adjust =ruff= to =black= / =yapf= if your project uses a
different formatter — must match the project's =make format=.)

* =:tangle= path resolves relative to the =.org= file

*Trap.* Paths like =:tangle ../../repos/<sub>/foo.py= are resolved
*from the =.org= file's directory*, NOT the project root. A =git mv=
of the =.org= silently breaks all of its tangle targets.

*Symptom.* You rename =lp/old-name/x.org= → =lp/new-name/x.org=.
Tangle emits files at unexpected paths (or fails), and
=.cache/tangle-map.tsv= goes stale.

*Workaround.* When moving a =.org=, also update every =:tangle=
path inside it. Then refresh the cache:

#+begin_example
make build-tangle-map
#+end_example

* Comma-escape =* = at column 0 inside =#+BEGIN_SRC=

*Trap.* A line starting with =* = (asterisk + space) at column 0
*anywhere* inside a =#+BEGIN_SRC python ... #+END_SRC= block —
including inside Python triple-quoted docstrings — is parsed by org
as a *headline* that silently terminates the enclosing src block.

*Symptom.* The tangler emits a partial / corrupted =.py=. Lines
after the bare-=*= line end up at top-level org or in the next
section's src block.

*Workaround.* Prepend a comma. Org strips it at tangle time so the
output =.py= is byte-identical to what the unescaped block would
have produced.

#+begin_example
,#+BEGIN_SRC python
,def foo():
,    """Docstring.

,    ,* Important note (escaped with leading comma).
,    """
,    pass
,#+END_SRC
#+end_example

The =block-bare-star-in-src.sh= hook enforces this — it rejects any
Edit / Write / MultiEdit that would *introduce* a new bare-=*=
violation. See =rules/lp-comma-escape-leading-star.md= for context.

* Use =:noweb yes= to split big classes / functions

*Trap.* A 200-line class in one =#+BEGIN_SRC= block is hard to read
and impossible to discuss section-by-section.

*Symptom.* The =.org= reads like a giant code listing instead of
a document.

*Workaround.* Split via noweb references. The tangler stitches them
back into one class body. See =rules/lp-noweb-for-big-blocks.md=
for the canonical pattern.

#+begin_example
,#+BEGIN_SRC python :noweb yes
class MyClass:
    <<__init__>>

    <<method-foo>>

    <<method-bar>>
,#+END_SRC
#+end_example

Each =<<__init__>>= etc. is defined in its own =#+NAME:='d block
with prose preamble.

* Byte-equivalence gate after tangle

*Trap.* It's easy to miss when an =.org= edit changes the tangled
output in ways you didn't intend (formatter ran differently, a
=:noweb= reference resolved to stale content, etc.).

*Symptom.* =git diff --stat python/= is non-empty after =make tangle=
when you only intended to change prose.

*Workaround.* After every tangle, =git diff --stat python/= MUST
show only the files you intended to change. Non-empty diff for
untouched modules = drift; investigate before committing.

#+begin_example
make tangle
git diff --stat python/    # MUST be empty (or match your intent)
#+end_example

* See also

- =../rules/python-literate-programming.md= — Python LP doctrine
- =../rules/lp-noweb-for-big-blocks.md= — noweb conventions
- =../rules/lp-comma-escape-leading-star.md= — org parser trap
- =../docs/tangle-map-schema.org= — reverse-lookup cache
